content: A 2025 meta-learning memory consolidation agent can rewire its own curiosity-driven exploration weights over time by treating unconsolidated episodic memories of novel environments as active learning training data, pruning low-value exploratory paths that never led to long-term task gains while consolidating high-value exploratory patterns into its permanent base policy. This turns the agent's intrinsic curiosity system from an unregulated exploration driver into a self-improving loop that bootstraps its own generalization over time.
